methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Blykalla, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Janne Wallenius and Peter Szakalos, is evaluated within a sector landscape comprising 3 public and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $60M+ to date.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, using the $60M total raised as a valuation proxy. This method yields $114M. Peer-median triangulation was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints at matching stage and valuation tier within the nuclear energy sector; while 8 same-sector peers exist, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer remained after filtering, falling short of the 3-company threshold required for reliable median estimation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $97M to $131M, centered on $114M. This range reflects the stage-progression anchor with ±15% bounds to account for typical early-stage variability. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking milestones, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital intensity requirements through commercial deployment. The single-method derivation underscores reliance on stage-based convention rather than market-tested peer comparables; future updates incorporating additional late-stage or exited nuclear venture transactions would strengthen confidence in this range.
